claim: Organizational failure in labor discipline allowed prohibited ignition-capable materials (angle grinder, flammable aerosol, synthetic oil) to be present in a high-methane zone, violating explosion safety protocols.
evidence: U-A1: angle grinder with 6 used disks found in conveyor drift; aerosol can marked 'toxic and flammable' on shearer; plastic bottle with synthetic oil in section 144; D-A5: no evidence of angle grinder use documented, but physical presence confirmed.
warrant: The presence of non-explosion-proof tools and flammable substances in a methane-prone area indicates a breakdown in supervision and enforcement of safety rules. These items should have been prohibited underground per standard safety regulations. Their presence reflects a systemic failure in labor discipline and daily oversight (OC-06).

claim: Failure to implement pre-drainage of the K2 companion seam constituted a critical organizational oversight in degasification planning, allowing uncontrolled methane release into the longwall goaf.
evidence: K-A2: K2 seam identified as primary methane source due to abutment pressure decompression; D-A1: no borehole pre-drainage of K2 seam despite 15 m3/t gas content; D-A3: isolation methane removal system only captured 34.1% of CH4, indicating incomplete control.
warrant: The absence of pre-drainage boreholes for a known, high-gas companion seam reflects a failure in hazard recognition and technical planning. This is a recurring risk in multi-seam mining operations and should have been addressed in project documentation and degasification design (OC-08, OC-07). Precedent PREC-2021-04 involved similar unmitigated methane sources.

claim: The mine's ventilation scheme was operated in violation of technical documentation by maintaining a combined 1K-N-N-vt system that created stagnant zones prone to methane accumulation, despite known design vulnerabilities.
evidence: D-A3: ventilation operated within design airflow but used a combined scheme creating stagnant sub-conveyor zones; K-A2: methane accumulated at floor level in sections 128-142 due to poor mixing; leakage coefficient 1.3 indicates inefficiency.
warrant: Operating a ventilation system with known structural vulnerabilities in methane-prone areas constitutes a violation of technical documentation (OC-05). The persistent use of a scheme that allows undiluted methane buildup in low-velocity zones suggests production priorities overrode safety considerations, a pattern seen in prior methane explosions.

claim: Management failed to recognize and act on the hazard posed by mechanical sparking from the armored face conveyor in methane-rich environments, despite manufacturer instructions and historical precedent.
evidence: K-A4: sparking observed on AFC; manufacturer instruction 9.2.1 requires compressed air ventilation of scraper chain when methane is present; sparking zone coincides with methane accumulation; 16% of Russian coal mine explosions attributed to conveyor sparking (2004–2019).
warrant: The failure to implement a known mitigation measure (compressed air ventilation) in a high-risk area indicates a systemic hazard recognition failure (OC-10). Historical data on conveyor sparking as a leading ignition source should have prompted procedural changes or engineering controls.

claim: Production control and supervision systems failed to detect or prevent unauthorized work activities and unsafe conditions, including the use of prohibited equipment and unmitigated methane accumulation zones.
evidence: U-A1: angle grinder, flammable aerosol, and synthetic oil found at ignition site; D-A6: CO spike at 02:24:20 preceded phone call by 5 minutes, indicating delayed response; no record of work permits or safety checks for hot work.
warrant: The presence of multiple ignition-capable items and the lack of immediate reporting indicate a breakdown in real-time supervision and production control (OC-01). Supervisors should have enforced work permit systems and conducted inspections to prevent such conditions.

claim: Work was conducted without proper authorization or safety briefing, as evidenced by the presence of unapproved tools and flammable materials in active mining areas.
evidence: U-A1: angle grinder with 6 used disks found in conveyor drift; aerosol can and synthetic oil bottle present; no documentation of approved hot work or material transport underground.
warrant: The use of tools and materials that require special permits (naryad-dopusk) without documented authorization indicates a violation of the work assignment system (OC-02). This reflects a culture where procedural compliance is weak and informal practices override formal safety protocols.
